title: Conditionals
description: Make programs that can make decisions

scenes.speech: Use if statements to let programs make choices!
scenes.title: Conditionals

scenes.speech: Code that only runs when condition is true!
scenes.title: Basic if Statement
scenes.content: if score >= 60: print('Pass!') runs only when the condition is True!
scenes.highlight: if condition: → runs only if True

scenes.speech: Code for when the condition is False!
scenes.title: else Statement
scenes.content: if ... else ... lets you say 'if this, do that, otherwise do this'!
scenes.highlight:
if condition: → True path
else: → False path

scenes.title: elif Statement
scenes.content: elif (else if) handles 3+ cases. Perfect for letter grade scoring!
scenes.highlight:
if 90+: A
elif 80+: B
elif 70+: C
else: D

scenes.speech: Learn the symbols used to build conditions!
scenes.title: Comparison Operators
scenes.content: == equal, != not equal, >, <, >=, <=. Use these to build condition expressions!

scenes.title: Logical Operators
scenes.content: and (both True), or (either True), not (reverse). Create complex conditions!

scenes.title: Ternary Operator
scenes.content: result = 'Pass' if score >= 60 else 'Fail' — write it in one line!

scenes.title: Nested Conditionals
scenes.content: You can put if statements inside if statements. Just don't go too deep or it gets hard to read!

scenes.speech: A new way to write conditions!
scenes.title: match Statement (Python 3.10+)
scenes.content: The match/case statement lets you write cleaner conditionals. Similar to switch in Java or C!

scenes.speech: You can now use conditional branches!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
